items.title: Wisselbeleid Ajax-trainer Grim gekraakt: 'Dan wordt het lastig'
items.summary: Ajax is te fragiel, oordeelde Khalid Boulahrouz bij Ziggo Sport na de Champions League-nederlaag tegen Olympiakos . Hij was tevens kritisch op het wisselbeleid van trainer Fred Grim.

items.title: Liverpool march into last 16 with thrashing of Qarabag
items.summary: Liverpool completed a resounding victory at Anfield as they thrashed Qarabag 6-0 to automatically reach the last 16 of the Champions League.

items.title: Benfica keeper scores stoppage time goal to seal win over Real Madrid
items.summary: Goalkeeper Anatoliy Trubin scores in the 98th minute to give Benfica a 4-2 win over Real Madrid - a goal which also secured Jose Mourinho's side a place in the last 16 play-offs of the Champions League on goal difference.

items.title: Ajax op Rapport: Dolberg en Steur lichtpuntjes
items.summary: Na de uitschakeling van Ajax in de Champions League, door een nederlaag tegen Olympiakos Piraeus , zijn de laatste rapportcijfers van het seizoen uitgedeeld. Kasper Dolberg en Sean Steur haalden als enigen een ruime voldoende.

items.title: VI Live: winst op Olympiakos was ook niet voldoende geweest voor Ajax
items.summary: Ajax nam het woensdagavond op tegen Olympiakos Piraeus in de Champions League, het werd een volgende Europese dreun voor de Amsterdammers. Er werd verloren van Olympiakos, wat resulteerde in uitschakeling. We volgden het duel in deze VI Live.

items.title: PSV op Rapport: Mauro Júnior ondanks rode kaart uitblinker, geen onvoldoendes
items.summary: Mauro Júnior was woensdagavond de grote uitblinker bij PSV. De Eindhovenaren verloren van Bayern München en zijn daardoor uitgeschakeld in de Champions League. De Braziliaan kreeg een rode kaart (twee keer geel), maar was wel de beste man van het veld.

items.title: VI Live: vijf Engelse clubs in topacht, Sporting verrast
items.summary: Woensdagavond staat de laatste speelronde van de competitiefase van de Champions League op het programma. Alle 36 clubs komen in actie, verdeeld over 18 wedstrijden. Voor PSV en Ajax hebben we een apart blog. In deze VI Live volg je de verrichtingen op de andere 16 velden.

items.title: Joao Pedro magic fires Chelsea into round of 16 with Napoli win
items.summary: Two sublime strikes from Joao Pedro fired Chelsea into the Champions League round of 16 as they finished sixth in the league phase table after a 3-2 win at Napoli.

items.title: Dembélé missed pen sees PSG settle for playoff
items.summary: Ousmane Dembélé had a penalty saved as holders Paris Saint-Germain were forced to settle for a Champions League playoff place following a draw with Newcastle.

items.title: USMNT's Tillman nets 2 as Bayer book playoff spot
items.summary: United States midfielder Malik Tillman scored an unusual though smart goal to give Bayer Leverkusen an early lead in the Champions League against Villarreal on Wednesday, then added a second to make it 2-0.

items.title: Rodman coaxes dance from Hayes in USWNT rout
items.summary: Trinity Rodman capped off the USWNT's 5-0 rout of Chile by coaxing coach Emma Hayes into a sideline dance, as a young American squad secured its fifth consecutive shutout Tuesday night.

items.title: Chelsea clinch top 8 with win, eliminate Napoli
items.summary: João Pedro led a Chelsea fightback that saw them through to the Champions League last 16 with two second-half goals to beat Napoli 3-2 at the Diego Maradona Stadium.

items.title: Champions League: Chelsea knock out Napoli; Benfic...
items.summary: Joao Pedro scored twice as Chelsea claimed a top-eight finish. Elsewhere, Benfica goalkeeper Anatoliy Trubin headed a late goal to send Jose Mourinho's side through.

items.title: João Pedro’s double dumps out Napoli as Chelsea avoid playoff with late win
items.summary: The onus was on Chelsea to silence the jibes about their lack of experience. Behind to a goal from Rasmus Højlund at half-time, they were facing the inconvenience of a two-legged playoff to reach the last 16 of the Champions League. Another lead had been thrown away and the momentum was with Napoli after they cancelled out a penalty from Enzo Fernández at a bouncing Stadio Diego Armando Maradona. Yet the night ended with a humiliating early exit for Napoli and another failure for Antonio Conte in Europe. The former Chelsea manager watched in despair as Cole Palmer came on to undo his depleted side with a devastating cameo. Palmer set up João Pedro for two outstanding goals as Liam Rosenior’s Chelsea finished in the top eight thanks to their first away win at this level in over three years. Continue reading...

items.title: Mohamed Salah back among the goals as Liverpool thrash Qarabag
items.summary: Liverpool are having to stomach “a very bad cocktail” of cheap goals against and wasted chances in the Premier League, according to Arne Slot. To extend the mixologist’s analogy, his team are enjoying life with a few margaritas in the Champions League. Slot’s team cruised into the last 16 for the second successive season with a demolition of Qarabag at Anfield. Mohamed Salah was among the goalscorers for the first time since accusing Liverpool of throwing him under the bus in December, while Alexis Mac Allister, Florian Wirtz, the influential Hugo Ekitiké and Federico Chiesa were also on the scoresheet. Continue reading...

items.title: Brave Newcastle fall short but Joe Willock stalls PSG’s progression as well
items.summary: On a chilly, fun, boisterous night in Paris, with the Champions League mega-table scrolling away in the background throughout a breathless 90 minutes, Newcastle produced a fine away performance at the home of the European champions. A 1-1 draw means Eddie Howe’s team will now enter the knockout phase in February, as had always seemed likely. Paris Saint-Germain will now join them there after some late score-ticker malarkey sent the reigning champions into the playoffs. Continue reading...

items.title: Kai Havertz back among the goals as Arsenal muscle past Kairat to finish top
items.summary: Arsenal’s challenge for the Premier League title may be showing some signs of wobbling but there are no such problems in the Champions League. An eighth successive victory – all by a margin of at least two goals – means Mikel Arteta’s side have become the first team to end the league phase with a 100% record since it was introduced last season. Whether that can translate into silverware in a competition that Arsenal have infamously never won and in which they only reached one final – way back in 2006 under Arsène Wenger – remains to be seen. But even with a second-string side that once again showed the strength in depth at Arteta’s disposal, completing the job against the bottom side was never in doubt even when Kairat equalised after an early goal from Viktor Gyökeres. Continue reading...

items.title: Kolo Muani fires Spurs through on return to Eintracht Frankfurt to offer Frank relief
items.summary: Randal Kolo Muani was lucky to be on the field. The Tottenham striker had suffered a moment of indescribable panic when the front right tyre on his Ferrari blew out on the motorway on Tuesday as he drove to Stansted airport for the team flight to Frankfurt. The car was a write-off but Kolo Muani walked away from it. He had to take a later flight to make this Champions League game and he was plainly determined to make his mark on it. Continue reading...

items.title: 'An unbelievable miss' - Korda calls for mixed-gender indoor golf league
items.summary: LPGA Tour star Nelly Korda has accused TGL organisers of making an "unbelievable miss" by choosing not to create a mixed-gender event for the indoor golf competition after announcing a separate women's edition to run alongside the already established men's version.

items.title: Flanagan: Leopardstown ground to be no issue for Marine Nationale
items.summary: Sean Flanagan is set to once again partner champion chaser Marine Nationale when he lines up at the Dublin Racing Festival this weekend, aiming to emulate the heroics of his Prestbury Park triumph back in March.
